mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS rises, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates consumer spending is shifting from durable goods to services, creating a resilient demand environment that supports positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect strong manufacturing and utility output, which correlates with robust corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO levels, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI inflation above 3% erodes real purchasing power and forces central banks to tighten policy, creating headwinds that produce negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation observations, or the negative correlation breaks down when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a broad-based labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and corporate hiring, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, or interaction effect is insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP exceeding 0.5% signals an economic contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of contraction quarters, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated industrial production levels during a supply-constrained regime reflect capacity utilization bottlenecks that eventually ease, allowing margin expansion and generating positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns turn negative when INDPRO is high during supply bottlenecks, or the positive signal reverses direction in this specific regime.
